questions.stem:
-Have you ever ______ to the Shenzhen Science Museum?
-Yes, I ______ there last month.
questions.explanation: 第一空考查现在完成时：have been to表示'去过某地已经回来'，have gone to表示'去了某地还没回来'，根据语境'我上个月去的'说明已经回来，用been。第二空有明确过去时间状语last month，必须用一般过去时went。解题技巧：看到'ever'想现在完成时，看到'last+时间'想一般过去时。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: The girl ______ is singing on the stage is my classmate. She won the first prize in the singing competition.
questions.explanation: 考查定语从句关系词。先行词the girl指人，且在从句中作主语（is singing），故用who或that。which指物，排除A；whom指人但作宾语，排除C；whose表示'谁的'，在从句中作定语，排除D。解题口诀：先行词是人作主语用who，作宾语用whom，表所属用whose；先行词是物用which/that。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: These old books ______ to the library tomorrow morning.
questions.explanation: 考查一般将来时的被动语态。主语books与return是被动关系（书被归还），且时间状语tomorrow morning表示将来，故用will be returned。A是主动语态，B是一般现在时被动，D是一般过去时，均不符合。解题技巧：' tomorrow'提示将来时，'书被还'提示被动，结构为will be done。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem:
-I wonder ______ Mary will come to the party tonight.
-If she ______, her parents will be angry.
questions.explanation: 第一空wonder后接宾语从句，表示'是否'用if或whether，排除C（that无'是否'含义）和D（when表时间，与答句逻辑不符）。第二空考查if引导的条件状语从句，遵循'主将从现'原则，主句will be angry（将来时），从句用一般现在时doesn't come。A选项won't come是将来时，违反'主将从现'。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: The ______ of the mountain is covered with snow all year round. It looks beautiful from far away.
questions.explanation: 考查名词词义辨析。根据'covered with snow all year round'（终年积雪）和'looks beautiful from far away'（远看很美）可知应是山的'顶部'top。foot山脚，side侧面，bottom底部，均不符合'终年积雪'这一地理特征。解题技巧：利用上下文线索'snow'和'from far away'推断位置。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: It is ______ for us to protect the environment. We should take action right now instead of waiting.
questions.explanation: 考查形容词辨析及语境理解。根据'We should take action right now'（我们应该立即行动）可知保护环境是'necessary必要的'。possible可能的，impossible不可能的，unnecessary不必要的，均与'立即行动'的紧迫语气不符。固定句型：It is necessary for sb to do sth。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: The old bridge is not strong enough to support heavy trucks. It may ______ if too many trucks cross it at the same time.
questions.explanation: 考查动词短语辨析。break down此处指'（机器、结构）垮塌、损坏'，符合'桥不够坚固'的语境。fall down侧重'（人）摔倒、（物）掉落'；turn down调低（音量）、拒绝；write down写下。解题技巧：break down多指机械故障或结构损坏，fall down多指失去平衡倒下。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.explanation: 考查形容词辨析及上下文语境。前文提到学校启动环保项目，此处指'起初一些学生认为改变长期习惯是不必要的'，与后文'他们继续到处扔垃圾'形成逻辑一致。easy容易的，interesting有趣的，possible可能的，均不如necessary能体现学生对环保态度的转变过程。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started a meaningful environmental project called "Green Campus". The main goal was to reduce waste and save energy in our daily school life, which would help protect the environment around us.

At first, some students thought it was not (8)______ to change their long-standing habits. They kept throwing rubbish everywhere and leaving lights on in empty classrooms after class. Some even forgot to turn off computers when they went home. However, after the headmaster gave us an inspiring speech about the importance of protecting the earth for future generations, things began to (9)______ gradually.

My class decided to (10)______ a special activity called "No Plastic Week". We promised to stop using plastic bottles and bags for seven days completely. It was quite difficult at the beginning, but we soon got used to bringing our own cups and cloth bags to school. By the end of the week, we had saved over 200 plastic items from being thrown away. We felt really proud of ourselves.

The project taught us that small actions can make a big (11)______ to the environment. Now, more and more students are actively joining the green movement. They have finally realized that everyone has an important role to (12)______ in making our planet cleaner and better for all living things.

questions.explanation: 考查动词辨析及逻辑关系。前文说学生不改变习惯，However转折后表示'情况开始逐渐改变'，change符合语境。happen发生，appear出现，disappear消失，均不能与'things'搭配表示'情况好转/改变'。解题技巧：However提示前后对比，前面是不改变，后面就是改变。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started a meaningful environmental project called "Green Campus". The main goal was to reduce waste and save energy in our daily school life, which would help protect the environment around us.

At first, some students thought it was not (8)______ to change their long-standing habits. They kept throwing rubbish everywhere and leaving lights on in empty classrooms after class. Some even forgot to turn off computers when they went home. However, after the headmaster gave us an inspiring speech about the importance of protecting the earth for future generations, things began to (9)______ gradually.

My class decided to (10)______ a special activity called "No Plastic Week". We promised to stop using plastic bottles and bags for seven days completely. It was quite difficult at the beginning, but we soon got used to bringing our own cups and cloth bags to school. By the end of the week, we had saved over 200 plastic items from being thrown away. We felt really proud of ourselves.

The project taught us that small actions can make a big (11)______ to the environment. Now, more and more students are actively joining the green movement. They have finally realized that everyone has an important role to (12)______ in making our planet cleaner and better for all living things.

questions.explanation: 考查动词辨析及固定搭配。organize an activity组织活动，是固定搭配。join参加（组织/活动），但通常说join in an activity或join an organization；attend出席（会议/课程）；visit参观（地点）。此处主语是My class，后接activity，organize最符合'班级发起活动'的语境。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:

At first, some students thought it was not (8)______ to change their long-standing habits. They kept throwing rubbish everywhere and leaving lights on in empty classrooms after class. Some even forgot to turn off computers when they went home. However, after the headmaster gave us an inspiring speech about the importance of protecting the earth for future generations, things began to (9)______ gradually.

The project taught us that small actions can make a big (11)______ to the environment. Now, more and more students are actively joining the green movement. They have finally realized that everyone has an important role to (12)______ in making our planet cleaner and better for all living things.

questions.explanation: 考查固定短语。make a difference是固定搭配，意为'有影响、起作用'。make a mistake犯错误，make a decision做决定，make a plan制定计划，均不符合句意'小行动能对环境产生大影响'。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:

questions.explanation: 考查固定搭配。play a role in...是固定短语，意为'在...中扮演角色/起作用'。take拿走，make制作，give给予，均不与role构成此搭配。句意：每个人都可以在让地球变得更清洁方面发挥作用。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.stem: How many hours do Shenzhen teenagers spend on screens daily on average according to the study?
questions.explanation: 细节理解题。根据文章首句'A recent study shows that teenagers in Shenzhen spend an average of 6.5 hours on screens every day'可知，深圳青少年平均每天花6.5小时在屏幕上。A选项1-2小时是使用教育app的时间，C和D未提及。解题技巧：题干中'average'对应原文'average'，直接定位首句找数字。
questions.label: 阅读-细节理解
questions.group_passage:
A recent study shows that teenagers in Shenzhen spend an average of 6.5 hours on screens every day. This includes time spent on smartphones, computers, and tablets for both study and entertainment.

Dr. Li, a local health expert, explains that excessive screen time can lead to several problems. "Many students complain about dry eyes and headaches after using devices for long periods," he says. "More importantly, spending too much time online may affect their sleep quality and face-to-face communication skills."

However, not all screen time is harmful. The study also found that students who use educational apps and online learning platforms for 1-2 hours daily often perform better in school. The key is to find a healthy balance between digital activities and other aspects of life.

To help students develop better habits, some schools have started "Screen-Free Hours" during lunch breaks. During this time, students are encouraged to play outdoor games, read physical books, or simply chat with friends. "At first, I felt anxious without my phone," says Bosco, a 14-year-old student. "But now I enjoy playing basketball with my classmates. It's more fun than staring at a screen."

The study suggests that parents should also set good examples by reducing their own screen time. Family activities like hiking or board games can provide excellent alternatives to digital entertainment.

questions.stem: The underlined word 'excessive' in Paragraph 2 probably means ______.
questions.explanation: 词义猜测题。根据后文' Many students complain about dry eyes and headaches'（许多学生抱怨眼睛干涩和头痛）可知，这是'过多的'screen time导致的问题。proper适当的，limited有限的，necessary必要的，均与'导致问题'的负面语境不符。解题技巧：利用因果线索，后文列举的都是负面影响，故excessive应表示'过量、过多'。
questions.label: 阅读-词义猜测
questions.group_passage:
A recent study shows that teenagers in Shenzhen spend an average of 6.5 hours on screens every day. This includes time spent on smartphones, computers, and tablets for both study and entertainment.

Dr. Li, a local health expert, explains that excessive screen time can lead to several problems. "Many students complain about dry eyes and headaches after using devices for long periods," he says. "More importantly, spending too much time online may affect their sleep quality and face-to-face communication skills."

However, not all screen time is harmful. The study also found that students who use educational apps and online learning platforms for 1-2 hours daily often perform better in school. The key is to find a healthy balance between digital activities and other aspects of life.

To help students develop better habits, some schools have started "Screen-Free Hours" during lunch breaks. During this time, students are encouraged to play outdoor games, read physical books, or simply chat with friends. "At first, I felt anxious without my phone," says Bosco, a 14-year-old student. "But now I enjoy playing basketball with my classmates. It's more fun than staring at a screen."

The study suggests that parents should also set good examples by reducing their own screen time. Family activities like hiking or board games can provide excellent alternatives to digital entertainment.

questions.stem: What can we infer from the passage?
options: A. All screen time is bad for teenagers.
B. Students should never use smartphones at school.
C. Educational apps can be helpful if used properly.
D. Bosco still hates playing basketball now.
questions.explanation: 推理判断题。根据第三段'students who use educational apps... for 1-2 hours daily often perform better in school'和'The key is to find a healthy balance'可推断，教育类app如果使用得当（时间适当）是有帮助的。A与第三段'not all screen time is harmful'矛盾；B过于绝对，文中未说'should never use'；D与最后Bosco说'now I enjoy playing basketball'矛盾。解题技巧：推理题要避免绝对化选项（all/never）和与原文直接矛盾的选项。
questions.label: 阅读-推理判断
questions.group_passage:
A recent study shows that teenagers in Shenzhen spend an average of 6.5 hours on screens every day. This includes time spent on smartphones, computers, and tablets for both study and entertainment.

Dr. Li, a local health expert, explains that excessive screen time can lead to several problems. "Many students complain about dry eyes and headaches after using devices for long periods," he says. "More importantly, spending too much time online may affect their sleep quality and face-to-face communication skills."

However, not all screen time is harmful. The study also found that students who use educational apps and online learning platforms for 1-2 hours daily often perform better in school. The key is to find a healthy balance between digital activities and other aspects of life.

To help students develop better habits, some schools have started "Screen-Free Hours" during lunch breaks. During this time, students are encouraged to play outdoor games, read physical books, or simply chat with friends. "At first, I felt anxious without my phone," says Bosco, a 14-year-old student. "But now I enjoy playing basketball with my classmates. It's more fun than staring at a screen."

The study suggests that parents should also set good examples by reducing their own screen time. Family activities like hiking or board games can provide excellent alternatives to digital entertainment.

questions.stem: What is the best title for this passage?
options: A. The Importance of Online Learning
B. How to Use Educational Apps
C. Teenagers and Screen Time: Finding Balance
questions.explanation: 主旨大意题。文章先讲青少年屏幕时间过长的问题，再讲合理使用教育app的好处，最后讲如何平衡屏幕时间与其他活动，核心是'青少年与屏幕时间：寻找平衡'。A只涉及第三段内容，片面；B也是局部信息；D文中未提及。解题技巧：最佳标题要涵盖全文主要内容，既要有screen time，又要有balance的概念。
questions.label: 阅读-主旨大意
questions.group_passage:
A recent study shows that teenagers in Shenzhen spend an average of 6.5 hours on screens every day. This includes time spent on smartphones, computers, and tablets for both study and entertainment.

Dr. Li, a local health expert, explains that excessive screen time can lead to several problems. "Many students complain about dry eyes and headaches after using devices for long periods," he says. "More importantly, spending too much time online may affect their sleep quality and face-to-face communication skills."

However, not all screen time is harmful. The study also found that students who use educational apps and online learning platforms for 1-2 hours daily often perform better in school. The key is to find a healthy balance between digital activities and other aspects of life.

To help students develop better habits, some schools have started "Screen-Free Hours" during lunch breaks. During this time, students are encouraged to play outdoor games, read physical books, or simply chat with friends. "At first, I felt anxious without my phone," says Bosco, a 14-year-old student. "But now I enjoy playing basketball with my classmates. It's more fun than staring at a screen."

The study suggests that parents should also set good examples by reducing their own screen time. Family activities like hiking or board games can provide excellent alternatives to digital entertainment.

options: A. However, you can make it easier with the right methods.
B. For example, use different folders for different subjects.
C. Teachers and senior students are good resources when you face difficulties.
D. This will help you build social connections and reduce stress.
E. Besides, you should study hard every day.
questions.explanation: 考查段落衔接。前文说'开始初中生活既令人兴奋又有挑战性'，后文说'这里有一些有用的建议'，中间需要过渡句承上启下。A项'然而，用正确的方法你可以让它变得更容易'符合逻辑，However转折承接'challenging'，引出下文的解决方法。B是举例，C讲求助，D讲好处，E讲学习，均不能承接'挑战'与'建议'之间的过渡。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Starting middle school can be both exciting and challenging for many students. (17)______ Here are some useful tips to help you get used to the new environment quickly.

First, try to be more organized. (18)______ Write down your homework and important dates in a notebook so you won't forget anything.

Second, don't be afraid to ask for help. (19)______ They have rich experience and are always willing to support you.

Finally, join some school clubs or sports teams. (20)______ You can make new friends who share similar interests with you.

Remember, everyone feels nervous at first. Just take your time and be yourself. Soon you'll find that middle school life is full of fun and surprises.

options: A. However, you can make it easier with the right methods.
B. For example, use different folders for different subjects.
C. Teachers and senior students are good resources when you face difficulties.
D. This will help you build social connections and reduce stress.
E. Besides, you should study hard every day.
questions.explanation: 考查逻辑关系。前句说'try to be more organized'（尽量有条理），后句说'Write down...'（写下作业和重要日期），中间需要具体举例说明如何做到organized。B项'例如，用不同的文件夹装不同的科目'是具体例子，衔接自然。A是过渡句，C讲求助，D讲好处，E与学习相关但不如B具体体现organized。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Starting middle school can be both exciting and challenging for many students. (17)______ Here are some useful tips to help you get used to the new environment quickly.

First, try to be more organized. (18)______ Write down your homework and important dates in a notebook so you won't forget anything.

Second, don't be afraid to ask for help. (19)______ They have rich experience and are always willing to support you.

Finally, join some school clubs or sports teams. (20)______ You can make new friends who share similar interests with you.

Remember, everyone feels nervous at first. Just take your time and be yourself. Soon you'll find that middle school life is full of fun and surprises.

options: A. However, you can make it easier with the right methods.
B. For example, use different folders for different subjects.
C. Teachers and senior students are good resources when you face difficulties.
D. This will help you build social connections and reduce stress.
E. Besides, you should study hard every day.
questions.explanation: 考查上下文衔接。前句说'don't be afraid to ask for help'（不要怕求助），后句'They have rich experience'（他们有丰富经验），They指代C项中的'Teachers and senior students'。C项说明向谁求助，与后文的They形成指代关系。A、B、D均不能被They指代。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Starting middle school can be both exciting and challenging for many students. (17)______ Here are some useful tips to help you get used to the new environment quickly.

First, try to be more organized. (18)______ Write down your homework and important dates in a notebook so you won't forget anything.

Second, don't be afraid to ask for help. (19)______ They have rich experience and are always willing to support you.

Finally, join some school clubs or sports teams. (20)______ You can make new friends who share similar interests with you.

Remember, everyone feels nervous at first. Just take your time and be yourself. Soon you'll find that middle school life is full of fun and surprises.

options: A. However, you can make it easier with the right methods.
B. For example, use different folders for different subjects.
C. Teachers and senior students are good resources when you face difficulties.
D. This will help you build social connections and reduce stress.
E. Besides, you should study hard every day.
questions.explanation: 考查因果关系。前句说'join some school clubs or sports teams'（参加社团或运动队），后句说'You can make new friends'（你能交到新朋友），中间需要说明参加活动的好处。D项'这将帮助你建立社交联系并减少压力'承上启下，This指代参加社团，build social connections对应make new friends。A、B、C均不能体现参加社团的好处。

questions.group_passage:

Second, don't be afraid to ask for help. (19)______ They have rich experience and are always willing to support you.

Finally, join some school clubs or sports teams. (20)______ You can make new friends who share similar interests with you.

Remember, everyone feels nervous at first. Just take your time and be yourself. Soon you'll find that middle school life is full of fun and surprises.
